message:
perf(reactor): reuse poll event buffers across poll() calls to eliminate per-poll allocations

All three reactor backends (kqueue, macOS kqueue, Windows IOCP) previously
allocated a fresh event buffer on every poll() call. This moves the buffer
into a Mutex<> field on the reactor struct and reuses it across calls,
only reallocating when the requested capacity diverges significantly from
the current capacity (less than target, or more than 4x target).

Changes per backend:
- kqueue.rs: Add poll_events: Mutex<PollingEvents> field, reuse with
  should_resize_poll_events() heuristic, explicit drop() before returning
- macos.rs: Same pattern with Mutex<Vec<libc::kevent>>, capacity-based
  resize check, explicit drop() to release lock before return
- windows.rs: Same pattern with Mutex<PollEvents>, matching resize logic

The DEFAULT_POLL_EVENTS_CAPACITY of 64 entries covers typical workloads
without oversizing. The 4x shrink threshold prevents unbounded growth
from transient spikes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
